prose: Fukui reactivity indices for pyridine were computed using DFT, tier standard (B3LYP/def2-TZVP) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The input geometry was from the provided .xyz file; the calculation was a single point (geometry not relaxed at DFT). Three electronic states were computed: neutral (N), cation (N−1, charge +1, multiplicity 2), and anion (N+1, charge −1, multiplicity 2). Mulliken partial-charge differences were used to derive the Fukui indices. Convergence checks passed: Σf⁺ = 1.0000 and Σf⁻ = 1.0000, confirming proper closure of the electron-donation/acceptance. The most electrophilic site is the nitrogen atom (f⁺ = 0.48423), and the most nucleophilic site is carbon atom 4 (f⁻ = 0.11826). No warnings or non-convergence issues were reported. The integrity verdict is trustworthy=true, status=ok.
